answer: Install Docker and discover what Docker is; learn the foundational concepts and workflows of Docker. The intended progression is to first install Docker and understand what it is, then move on to learning its foundational concepts and workflows.

Four distinct types of cookies are described. The order of their presentation from most to least restrictive in terms of user control is:
1. Necessary cookies
2. Functionality and personalisation cookies
3. Performance cookies
4. Advertising cookies

Blocking 'Functionality cookies' may impact the user experience on the Docker documentation site if you do not allow these cookies, as some or all of the services that provide enhanced functionality and personalisation may not function properly.

Analytics cookies allow the website to count visits and traffic sources to measure and improve site performance, while advertising cookies are set by advertising partners to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites.

If you do not allow Advertising cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising. If you do not allow Analytics cookies, we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
